programme.title: Romancing the Stone: The Golden Ages of British Sculpture
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke examines sculpture in the 20th century, an era of innovation, scandal and creativity. He examines the artistic revolution spearheaded by Eric Gill and Jacob Epstein, which paved the way for the work of Gilbert and George and Damien Hirst. He also explores the art forms rise in popularity, from First World War memorials to the landmark work of Antony Gormley and Rachel Whiteread

programme.title: Face the Music
programme.desc: Joseph Cooper invites viewers to match their musical wits against panellists Joyce Grenfell, Robin Ray and Richard Baker. With guest musician Alfredo Campoli. First broadcast in 1976

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The historian treks through the southern fjords of Norway to find the stunning Oseberg ship. This lost 1,000-year-old vessel transformed the fierce reputation of the Vikings and became a symbol of a nations fight for freedom

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor discovers how carving up the landscape in order to build canals helped further mans understanding of the earth below. William Smith, a surveyor on the Somerset Coal Canal, discovered a way of ordering layers of rocks and eventually created the first geological map of England and Wales. His ideas about the age and formation of the earth were developed long before Darwin published his theories of evolution

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - Swansea and Llansteffan
programme.desc: In the first of two special programmes originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth, Derek Brockway strolls around the poets home town of Swansea. Accompanied by actor Adrian Metcalfe, Derek sets off from Dylans childhood home on Cwmdonkin Drive and walks through the citys parks and down to the seafront, before visiting some of the writers haunts in later life. He then heads west to the Llansteffan peninsula where his guide, artist Osi Rhys Osmond, leads him through the countryside that was home to many of Dylans relatives. They include his aunts farm, which would provide the inspiration for the poem Fernhill

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth visits a stately home, where she naturally tries her hardest to meet the lord of the manor - only to be thwarted by the untimely arrival of Onslow and Daisy. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy Lumsden meets Annette at his amateur dramatics society, but all his attempts to court her are ruined by his overbearing and tyrannical mother. First episode of the comedy, starring Ronnie Corbett and Barbara Lott

programme.title: Simon Schama Remembers - A History of Britain
programme.desc: Simon Schama looks back on his ambitious series, remembering the long debates over what to include and the search for apt locations. He talks of his inspirations - Kenneth Clark and Alistair Cook - and his drive to make history accessible to wider audiences

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Historian Simon Schama charts the 4,000 years prior to the 1000AD, from Stone Age Orkney and through four centuries of Roman occupation to King Alfreds ultimate victory over the Vikings, which laid the foundations for the Anglo-Saxon kingdom later subjugated by the Norman invaders

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: In the red-light district of Kolkata, a group of women reclaim their voices through the power of storytelling. Given unprecedented access, cameras follow Rabin, Bilkis, Afsara and other sex-working mothers who navigate the narrow lanes of Kalighat, an area operating as an independent brothel district. Together, they form a grassroots film collective, producing videos that reflect the realities and challenges of their community

programme.title: Border Country: The Story of Britains Lost Middleland
programme.desc: For historian Rory Stewart, the building of Hadrians Wall in the second century was the single most important event in Britains history. In the first of a two-part documentary, the MP for Penrith and the Border explores the landscape, oral history and archaeology of the region the wall runs through, examines the impact of Romes occupation and departure, and reveals how the kingdom of Northumbria was born

programme.title: Made by Machine: When AI Met the Archive
programme.desc: Hannah Fry and a virtual host present a new way of making television as the BBCs Research and Development uses Artificial intelligence to delve into the treasures of the BBC Archive. Computers trawl through more than quarter of a million shows using a variety of machine-learning techniques, and are let loose to create new mini BBC Four-style programmes-within-a-programme

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor discovers how carving up the landscape in order to build canals helped further mans understanding of the earth below. William Smith, a surveyor on the Somerset Coal Canal, discovered a way of ordering layers of rocks and eventually created the first geological map of England and Wales. His ideas about the age and formation of the earth were developed long before Darwin published his theories of evolution

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - Swansea and Llansteffan
programme.desc: In the first of two special programmes originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth, Derek Brockway strolls around the poets home town of Swansea. Accompanied by actor Adrian Metcalfe, Derek sets off from Dylans childhood home on Cwmdonkin Drive and walks through the citys parks and down to the seafront, before visiting some of the writers haunts in later life. He then heads west to the Llansteffan peninsula where his guide, artist Osi Rhys Osmond, leads him through the countryside that was home to many of Dylans relatives. They include his aunts farm, which would provide the inspiration for the poem Fernhill

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Exploring the artificial intelligence required to replace human drivers, focusing on issues including the ethics of driverless car crashes and the impact on jobs. Despite the glossy PR and positives such as reduced exhaust emissions, the reality is that the future of driverless cars might not be all good news

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of Englands Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an major challenge, but connecting the powerhouses of Yorkshire and Lancashire was a great incentive at the time of the Industrial Revolution. Liz considers the strategic considerations and implications of this massive engineering project

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - New Quay and Laugharne
programme.desc: Derek Brockway presents the second of two specials originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth. First he travels to the seaside town of New Quay on Cardigan Bay, where the poet and writer lived with wife Caitlin during the Second World War. Jeff Towns, chairman of the Dylan Thomas Society, joins Derek to explain how Thomas was inspired to write some of his greatest work during this era. For his final walk, to Laugharne, the presenter is accompanied by Thomass grandaughter Hannah Ellis, who reveals the significance of the town to both her grandparents and her family today

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Plants
programme.desc: Part two of two. Exploring how technical developments are enabling people to see the world of plants in greater detail, allowing insight into their remarkable abilities to adapt and survive

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The story of a giant otter cub rescued and raised by Brazilian naturalist Carolina Vargas. The creature has to be bottle fed, learn how to hunt fish and be taught how to survive the predators of the worlds largest wetland, the Pantanal in South America

programme.title: Tribe
programme.desc: Bruce Parry treks into the jungles of Sarawak, Borneo, in search of the last nomadic Penan, a tribe of hunter-gatherers whose forest home is in the process of being cut down around them. Their traditional way of life is about to disappear

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.sub-title: Bernard Hill Remembers - Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: The late actor Bernard Hill, who famously played the character of Yosser Hughes, reflects on the acclaimed drama about life in recession-hit Britain during the Thatcher era. He looks back on how he managed to get the role, why it struck such a chord with the public and what he hopes contemporary audiences will take away from the series

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Alan Bleasdales influential Liverpool-set drama, originally shown in 1982. Chrissie and his friends sign on at the benefit office before heading to a construction site for some cash-in-hand work - but investigators are on their trail. However, mistakes are made on both sides, with tragic consequences. Starring Michael Angelis, Bernard Hill and Alan Igbon

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Dixie continues to sign on the dole while working nights as a security guard on the docks - and when a Department of Employment investigator calls, his wife is convinced they are being spied on. Meanwhile, the former foreman comes under pressure to take bribes for allowing goods to be smuggled off ships on his watch. Second part of Alan Bleasdales drama, starring Tom Georgeson

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Chrissie and his wife Angie are driven to despair over their financial problems, to say nothing of their constant hounding from the Department of Employment investigators. Starring Michael Angelis, Julie Walters and Jean Boht

programme.title: Tribe
programme.desc: Bruce Parry treks into the jungles of Sarawak, Borneo, in search of the last nomadic Penan, a tribe of hunter-gatherers whose forest home is in the process of being cut down around them. Their traditional way of life is about to disappear

programme.title: Canals: The Making of a Nation
programme.desc: Liz McIvor tells the story of the early canal builders who struggled with the rugged terrain of Englands Pennine hills. Creating a network of canals in this landscape was an major challenge, but connecting the powerhouses of Yorkshire and Lancashire was a great incentive at the time of the Industrial Revolution. Liz considers the strategic considerations and implications of this massive engineering project

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.sub-title: Weatherman Walking: Dylans Walks - New Quay and Laugharne
programme.desc: Derek Brockway presents the second of two specials originally shown to celebrate the centenary of Dylan Thomass birth. First he travels to the seaside town of New Quay on Cardigan Bay, where the poet and writer lived with wife Caitlin during the Second World War. Jeff Towns, chairman of the Dylan Thomas Society, joins Derek to explain how Thomas was inspired to write some of his greatest work during this era. For his final walk, to Laugharne, the presenter is accompanied by Thomass grandaughter Hannah Ellis, who reveals the significance of the town to both her grandparents and her family today

programme.title: King of Thieves (2018)
programme.desc: A thief pulls together a band of misfit criminals to plot the biggest bank heist in British history. The crooks manage to escape with around £200 million worth of stolen jewels and money, but when police are called to the scene and the investigation starts, the cracks between the eccentric gang members begin to show, as they row over how to share the goods and become increasingly distrustful of each other. Crime drama based on the Hatton Garden heist, starring Michael Caine, Michael Gambon, Francesca Annis, Jim Broadbent, Ray Winstone and Tom Courtenay

Host Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings and also gets the chance to take a look at a magnificent trophy that puts this town on the map - the Cheltenham Gold Cup

Bear tasks them with keeping an ember alight while gathering fuel for a signal fire, while relationship therapist Simone Bose talks to them about the hurt theyve both experienced

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus is cooking for the Mayor of St Remys sister so he researches the origins of local produce to find culinary inspiration. There is one simple ingredient he has in mind - the worldwide staple rice - hitting upon the variety Camargue red which is grown just down the road. He chooses to pair it with pistachios in a salad - but will it impress his VIP guest?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Devon as she helps three generations escape from the West Midlands to the West Country - whether they end up all under one roof or side by side. Tasked with the viewings are Matt and his father-in-law Mark, who will then report back to their wives. Away from the search, Nicki also textile designer who swapped city life for rural creativity and even has a go at weaving on a handloom herself

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Hairstylist and singer Amanda needs a proper space for both her salon and music room, while her daughter Evie needs storage. Mark Wright, Tommy Walsh and Maxine Dwyer come to the rescue, reconfiguring Amandas salon into a multipurpose space with different work areas and soundproofing for her music. They aso transform Evies room with new shelving

They are guided by Paul Laidlaw, Margie Cooper and Tim Medhurst - but who will get to be top of the profit-making pops?

programme.title: Pointless

Elsewhere, Phil tries to convince Julie to call Clare, but she puts her foot down, and Howie is determined to make a difference

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Marcus Wareing, Monica Galetti and Matt Tebbutt present the last week of heats, for which they are joined by 2018 finalist Matthew Ryle, who sets the skills tests. Known for his French classic cookery, Matthew asks the first two chefs to take on his red mullet with braised fennel, citrus salad and blood orange hollandaise, before the second two serve up roasted poussin with peppercorn sauce and a potato garnish of their choosing

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mei Li reveals her life is at risk because she is a pro-democracy protester who organised a demonstration to free a political prisoner. Kit finds the arrest warrant for Mei Li, which states there is a bounty of $1 million for her capture, leading the team to wonder if they are looking at state-sponsored murder. However, the case takes an unexpected turn with the death of a second victim, while Burns reveals the identity of the dead woman originally thought to have been Mei Li. But as Nikki follows up further evidence, she soon finds herself in danger

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.sub-title: Derry Girls: Decades of Domestic Abuse
programme.desc: Figures show that on average one in four women living in Northern Ireland will experience domestic abuse in their lifetime. In 1976, two women armed with a bucket and a ladder broke into a building in Londonderry and opened the citys first refuge for women and children fleeing abuse. Five decades on, Foyle Womens Aid remains an ongoing place of safety. Jennifer OLeary uncovers some of their stories as Spotlight investigates a service shaped by the violence of others

programme.title: Ben Youngs Investigates: How Safe Is Rugby?
programme.desc: Englands most capped rugby union player explores if there is a link between concussion and long-term brain health in the wake of his former teammate Lewis Moodys motor neurone disease diagnosis. Moody discusses his life since his diagnosis in October last year, and Youngs also talks to Steve Thompson, who reveals he can no longer remember winning the 2003 World Cup and shares the challenges of living with dementia. The presenter then looks at what is being done by the sports governing bodies to ensure safety in the game, from new technologies to strengthened protocol

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Toy experts Julie Tatchell and Amanda are presented with a threadbare teddy bear with an extraordinary past, having been given to the owners mother, a nurse, by Hollywood songwriter Sharon Sheeley - immediately after shed survived the car crash that killed her boyfriend - 1950s rock n roll star Eddie Cochran. The other items are a cherished garden bench given to the owner by her late husband, a camera used to capture images of the Second World War and a 1940s fishing chart that helped the original owner - a Grimsby trawlerman - navigate the North Sea for more than 50 years

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Former finalist Matthew Ryle is back to challenge the next four contestants skills, asking them to re-create two of his recipes - raviolo stuffed with pea and ricotta, alongside a cheesy pepper sauce, and poached pear mille-feuille with a hazelnut praline cream. Plus, Aston Villa v Chelsea, Fulham v West Ham United, Manchester City v Nottingham Forest, Bournemouth v Brentford, Everton v Burnley, Leeds United v Sunderland and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Liverpool

programme.title: The TikTok Effect
programme.desc: What connects amateur sleuths turning up at crime scenes, anti-social behaviour in UK schools and French riots? This film finds evidence they are all examples of TikTok frenzies. The BBCs disinformation and social media correspondent Marianna Spring has spent the last year investigating unusual behaviour on TikTok spilling out into the real-world and her presents her findings

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Documentary following boxer Tommy Furys return to the ring. Tommy is at his lowest point personally and professionally, living alone in a house that was meant to be his and partner Molly-Maes forever home. His role as a father to baby Bambi brings both joy and pressure as he tries to convince Molly-Mae he is a changed man. After finally getting the medical all-clear to fight again, Tommy and his trainer and father John prepare for his return to the ring against MMA fighter Darren Till. Team Fury travels to the press conference - with John promising to be on his best behaviour

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy prepares for his first gym appearance since announcing his comeback, but with fighter Darren Till throwing threats and chaos into the mix, trainer John pulls Tommy out of the fight claiming he is unwilling to risk his sons future career. Its a crushing blow for Tommy, whos desperate for momentum and purpose. Amid the pressure, theres a flicker of hope - Tommy and partner Molly-Mae Hague are spotted together on New Years Eve, with a kiss that hints at a brighter future

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys fight for redemption continues, but this time, its a battle for his own mind. Struggling to secure a new opponent, he knows a comeback fight is crucial for his mental health. Meanwhile, Molly-Maes new series trailer drops, pushing Tommy to face his own truths in the toughest interview of his life, where he opens up about his battle with alcohol addiction. Back in training, hope returns as a potential fight looms, leading him and his father John to a career-defining meeting

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy is hopeful that he could be fighting again soon, and he and Molly-Mae are spending more time together. Then, disaster strikes when KSI cancels the night when Roman was due to be in the fighting, claiming illness. John is furious, but decides the boys need cheering up so tells them he is taking them on a camping trip. However, neither Tommy nor Roman share their fathers love of the outdoors

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but theres a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasnt reached Tommys account

programme.title: Thirteen
programme.desc: Drama about a woman who escapes and returns home after being held captive in a cellar for 13 years. The police face a desperate rush to confirm her identity when news of her escape reaches the press, and while her parents are overjoyed to have her home, her sister fears she may be an imposter. Meanwhile, the police search the house she claimed to have been held in - and discover evidence that contradicts her story. Starring Jodie Comer

programme.title: Thirteen
programme.desc: Carne and Merchant find themselves working with a new team from Scotland Yard, and the need to get to the truth becomes more pressing than ever. Merchant comes up with a bold approach in making Ivy relive her ordeal, but her response under pressure leaves the detective convinced she is lying. Ivy turns to Tim for support, only to suffer a crushing betrayal

programme.title: Thirteen
programme.desc: Ivy tries to reconnect with her sister, but a mysterious letter which seems to be from Mark White threatens their reconciliation. Carne tries to get Ivy to open up about her captivity, but is forced to take drastic measures when she refuses to cooperate. As doubts about Ivys story grow, Merchant returns to Whites house, and makes a shocking discovery

programme.title: Thirteen
programme.desc: Ivy is arrested for perverting the course of justice after her DNA is found in the cellar, and she withdraws completely while being questioned as she realises she has lost Carnes trust. Christina begs the police to allow her to speak to her daughter, and persuades Ivy to reveal the shocking truth behind her disappearance

programme.title: Thirteen
programme.desc: The Moxam family is plunged into the same horror they faced 13 years previously, leaving Ivy faced with the prospect of risking her own life to save Phoebe - and being confronted with the memory of her earlier ordeal. Carne and Merchant try to find a way to ensure her safety, but have underestimated Mark White, putting themselves in danger

programme.title: The Kim Kardashian Diamond Heist
programme.desc: The inside story of the 2016 robbery and its impact, with those close to the case revealing details about the crime for the first time. Featuring interviews with friends of the family, police officers and journalists who have followed the case, this documentary recalls the fateful night in Paris when the US reality TV star was robbed of millions of dollars worth of jewellery at gunpoint

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the Indian Ocean, searching for an underwater giant that can weigh as much as a double-decker bus, and trying to spot the worlds fastest fish

programme.title: Deadly 60

programme.title: Dragons Den

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The candidates face a floristry challenge as Lord Sugar tasks them with selling flowers to the public and creating ambitious floral installations for corporate clients. While business blooms for one team, the other faces a bunch of problems

programme.sub-title: Did Your Dirty Words Comes Out to Play?
programme.desc: At Evan and Brees rehearsal dinner in the present day, tensions swirl beneath the celebrations. Lucy sneaks off to hook up with Max, deliberately luring Stephen to catch her. Flashbacks reveal messy Thanksgiving drama when a snowed-in campus forces everyone to Evans for dinner, Thriller, starring Grace Van Patten, Branden Cook and Catherine Missal

programme.title: Tell Me Lies
programme.desc: Lucy and Bree attend a professors Christmas party, while Lucy makes a rash decision that affects Pippa

programme.title: Tell Me Lies
programme.sub-title: Dont Struggle Like That, or I Will Only Love You More
programme.desc: Its the end of term and Christmas celebrations change everything for everyone - yet again

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: After a drunken mistake, Nina must battle a number of different challenges to make up for her error, but the damage may already have been done despite her best efforts

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: Nina hopes a work event might be just the distraction she needs, but she quickly realises there is more on the line than just winning Shanes fundraising competition

programme.title: Dinosaur
programme.desc: After so many misunderstandings, Nina knows her true feelings and wants to make things right, but a family emergency might stop her from telling Lee the truth

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: Comedian Jayde Adams is on hand to help as for one night only the queens form comedy double acts for RuPauls Fringe Festival. But who will have the last laugh? programme.title: Official Secrets (2019)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the publication of a leaked GCHQ memo in the run-up to the Iraq war. The leak had devastating consequences for whistleblower Katharine Gun, who was put on trial for breaching the Official Secrets Act, and the journalists who were determined to unmask a conspiracy. Starring Keira Knightley and Matt Smith

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Hamza Yassin is on the west coast of Scotland in search of one of the UKs rarest marine giants - the flapper skate. This ancient species has been gliding through our waters for millions of years, but today, it teeters on the edge of extinction. Hamza joins the team using cutting-edge science and a network of sea anglers to help bring the flapper skate back from the brink. Plus, marine marvels from the Countryfile archives

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A cast member is poisoned during a performance of The Tempest after drinking from a bottle of red wine with a lethal quantity of cyanide in, but the team are left confused as to how two other actors drank from the same bottle and emerged unscathed. Mervin is informed that his half-brother Solomon is set to inherit everything from their deceased mother and he wont answer any calls

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Freya is struggling with her modelling career. Her dreams of relaunching herself as an artist in London seem to be slowly fading away. Attempting to quit and regain some sense of control over her life and ambitions, she has met, with more than a little hysteria from her agents, the Olivias. They also spot, to their shock, an engagement ring on her finger. Starring Kyla Harris

programme.desc: Nina Wadia presents an appeal on behalf of Breakthrough T1D, a charity funding research to help cure, treat and prevent type 1 diabetes

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A couple with a 22-year age gap who struggle to agree on how to parent their seven children are challenged to take on a blindfolded river walk to get to camp. Then for their second challenge, one of them must climb a stack of crates and the other a rope ladder, before they both make a leap of faith for a reward. In their campfire chat, mediator Dave gets to the heart of their parenting clashes and helps them find a way to carve out time for themselves

programme.desc: Marcus sets out to discover what simple sweet treats are on offer in Provence. The chef and his neighbour Emilie cook a caramel quince compote, before Marcus learns the importance of almonds in the regions most famous sweet, Calisson

He chooses to pair it with pistachios in a salad - but will it impress his VIP guest?

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Viki Knott, Ian Humphries and Moses Otunla search the antiques markets of Pezenas for hidden treasures they know their online followers and loyal customers will love. Moses finds a mid-century desk he thinks will be perfect for his London clients who have deep pockets and small spaces. A large box containing more than 200 patterned 19th-century tiles catches Vikis eye as she thinks of the profits she could make selling them individually. Old-school dealer Ian has his sights set on a window seat in the style of 19th-century designer Gillows before he then sets the group the challenge of hunting for a cool and quirky piece

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers evidence of the German occupation of the Jersey village of Gorey and the role it played in defending the UK. He meets local historian Phillip Marett, who takes him to the gun tower from where the occupying soldiers could have watched the Battle of Normandy, and hears a first-hand recollection of the liberation of the island

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The crew embarks on a journey through the Mediterranean Sea, whose coasts are among the most densely populated in the world, to explore the profound effect man has had on the body of water. Paul Rose and the team also visit the remains of a Roman shipwreck and, under the cover of night, dive the Straits of Messina, hoping to find and film the sixgill shark - one of the largest predatory sharks in the world

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Nick Davies scour the crowd at Tilbury Docks in Essex to find objects to take off to auction, while Paul Martin explores the history of the docks

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of the West Midlands town of Dudley

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs compete for Scotland with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry. The dishes are judged by Michelin-starred chef Adam Handling, and include a leek and potato veloute inspired by Trainspotting and an aubergine baba ganoush in honour of Limbo. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits the north of Britain to look at the most fascinating archaeology uncovered in the region over the course of 2021. In North Yorkshire, a team of community volunteers are digging down beneath Richmond Castle. On the northern tip of the Orkney Islands, the dig diary cameras are present to witness archaeologists racing to unearth rare discoveries from an endangered Neolithic tomb before it erodes into the sea. And 10 miles east of Edinburgh, volunteers are hunting for evidence of Scotlands oldest railway

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: The journalist finds out what the future holds for Europe in a world where it can no longer fully rely on America, beginning by visiting Italy and Germany to explore how theyre changing in a turbulent new world. In the Tuscan hills, she joins Italian paratroopers on a Nato exercise with 1,500 soldiers from seven different countries, then she heads to Germany, where she visits a Volkswagen car factory to learn how theyre being affected by both Russia and China. And in Germanys far south, not far from Munich, she meets a family who are building a house with a nuclear bunker

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: RAF Saves Trapped Cow and Police Catch Poaching Suspect
programme.desc: Police uncover shocking evidence of animal cruelty linked to a series of farmland poaching attacks in Worksop, Nottinghamshire, and the RAF tackle a challenging rescue when Beauty the cow becomes trapped at the bottom of a steep gorge in Gilsland, Cumbria. Paul Colgan joins a rural crime officer using forensic DNA technology to mark valuable machinery in Ellerton, East Yorkshire

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Return of the programme following investigations into serious crimes. When 12-year-old Leo Ross is fatally stabbed in Birminghams Trittiford Mill Park on his way home from school, police suspect a 14-year-old, but need forensics to prove he is the killer. There is little information about whether Leo was targeted, and finding a murder weapon in such a vast area is an immediate challenge. The park has numerous pathways, an area of marshland, and no CCTV coverage., but bodycam footage from first responders proves to be crucial

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian returns with her travelogue in which she tracks down the worlds most endangered jobs and crafts. She begins in Greece, where she meets the last monk in a monastery, a family of church bell-makers, and villagers who communicate by whistling to one another

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: The candidates face a floristry challenge as Lord Sugar tasks them with selling flowers to the public and creating ambitious floral installations for corporate clients. While business blooms for one team, the other faces a bunch of problems

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the flowers task, the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Lord of the Flies
programme.desc: Ralph jumps into action when violence escalates, then realises with horror that it is his turn to be hunted. Jack Thornes adaptation of William Goldings novel about schoolboys stranded on a deserted island, starring Winston Sawyers and Lox Pratt

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: The Boy Who Tried to Kill the Queen
programme.desc: The mathematician explores stories emerging from the world of AI, meeting individuals whose lives have been transformed for better or worse by this new technology. Hannah begins by looking at the story of teenager Jaswant Singh Chail, who in 2021 began a relationship with an AI chatbot called Sarai. Over three weeks, they exchanged 5,000 messages and declared their love for one another and encouraged by Sarai, Jaswant broke into Windsor Castle with a crossbow on Christmas Day in an attempt to assassinate Elizabeth II

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A 28-year-old man is embarrassed by his fathers immature behaviour, so Bear sets them a water-collecting task to help them discover the power of teamwork, and an epic rescue mission in the dramatic landscape of a north Wales quarry. In a conversation with psychologist Tara, they share their feelings about their relationship

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: The Battle of the Pop Stars
programme.desc: Compilation episode following chart stars Peter Andre, Martin Kemp and Sinitta as they swap the studio for the saleroom to take part in the antiques buying challenge. They are guided by Paul Laidlaw, Margie Cooper and Tim Medhurst - but who will get to be top of the profit-making pops?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown helps a couple wanting space for animals to swap urban Gloucestershire for rural Herefordshire and also visits former escapees who now run a thriving organic farm

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Further coverage of lions, leopards and cheetahs in the Okavango Delta. The Xudum lion pride is now over 40 strong, and its mums must hunt around the clock to feed their growing cubs. The pride males Big Toe and Madumo are missing, leaving the most vulnerable cubs unprotected from dangerous intruder male lions

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: Adi Higham, James Gooch and Jacqui Harries head to the market in Villeneuve-les-Avignon to find some antique treasures for their loyal customers and followers. James finds some vintage skittles that he thinks will help him get one over on toy lover Adi who finds some alarming looking eel forks. Jacqui is drawn to a portable French Gramophone. James then sets the dealers the task of finding an 18th century treasure

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Yorkshire coastal village of Flamborough and discovers its historic chalk buildings. Local historian and archaeologist Andrew Jones shows Ben around Flamborough Tower. Constructed in 1674 and standing 87 feet tall, the tower was built with the purpose of improving ship to shore navigation but would ultimately be used by local law enforcers in a bid to prevent smuggling

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team explores the Arctic Ocean, one of the most hostile environments on Earth, yet one that plays a crucial part in controlling the climate of the planet. Paul Rose and the crew dive beneath the polar ice cap to examine how the ice, the dominant feature of the ocean, is shrinking and how this could affect the rest of the world

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Michael Baggot and David Fletcher examine items belonging to members of the public in Bexhill-on-Sea, East Sussex, including an art deco childrens clock and a painting that could be worth thousands of pounds. Paul Martin visits the site of the Battle of Hastings

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills ahead of double points Friday

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Gladiator Apollo, on their knowledge of Falkirk

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry, including venison loin smoked in gunpowder tea inspired by Skyfall and a frangipane custard tart in honour of Brave. Veteran judge Adam Handling helps to decide who deserves to say and who should go home. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Film-maker Chris Terrill captures an intimate and personal portrait of life on board ultra-modern super aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth, as its crew learns the intricacies of a warship that will change the way Britain goes to war. In this first instalment, early 2016 sees sailors begin to arrive in Rosyth dockyard in Scotland where the ship is still in construction, but nearing completion. Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors are assembling to take the prototype warship to sea for the first time. These trials herald what should be 50 years of service

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: Documentary about the fate of photojournalists John Cantlie and James Foley, who were kidnapped in Syria in 2012. The pair had gone out to the Middle East to chase a scoop about foreign fighters, only for their reporting to draw the attention of extremists - and what began as a tense investigation spiralled into a dangerous encounter

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Tensions run high as Jo and Freya meet for the first time in months. Both want to reconcile, but neither wants to lose ground. Meanwhile, at the behest of the Olivias, Abe has been roped into staging his and Freyas surprise proposal as the first big piece of content for their disability wedding and bridal influencer campaign. Starring Kyla Harris and Elena Samuel

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBCs world affairs editor reflects on the weeks major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: Fifty years ago an Australian preacher set up a religious community in New Zealand known as Gloriavale. Today it is home to 650 people who live a closeted existence away from the rest of society. This documentary examines the dark secrets at the heart of this Christian sect and the allegations that its followers are indoctrinated to an oppressive existence for life

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on what life was like for the second and third-generation members of Gloriavale, growing up in a world where every decision is made for them. New Zealand journalist Melanie Reid recounts her experience of going undercover inside the community in the 1990s, and the work she did to try to stop the abuse she discovered

programme.title: Escaping Utopia
programme.desc: The drama documentary charts the impact of the loss of Gloriavales charismatic leader Hopeful Christian and his successors struggle to keep the community running. Meanwhile, an underground network helps one member leave, while two former children of Gloriavale expose one of its most shocking secrets

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.desc: Claire tries to charm a doctor into sharing details, while Anna fixates on whether it is possible to turn right on Harcourt Street. Maggie arranges a place for Rachel at Cloisters, and although she is at first reluctant, she eventually agrees. As Anna waits for updates, the weight of uncertainty begins to settle in and she struggles to find the strength to face what lies ahead

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A couple whose marriage was pushed to the brink when the menopause altered the wifes attitude to her relationship try to reconnect through a problem-solving challenge. Bear tasks them with keeping an ember alight while gathering fuel for a signal fire, while relationship therapist Simone Bose talks to them about the hurt theyve both experienced

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Devon as she helps three generations escape from the West Midlands to the West Country - whether they end up all under one roof or side by side. Tasked with the viewings are Matt and his father-in-law Mark, who will then report back to their wives. Away from the search, Nicki also textile designer who swapped city life for rural creativity and even has a go at weaving on a handloom herself

programme.title: Historys Greatest Myths
programme.desc: How conflict has reshaped nations and devastated generations. Wars dominate history, but the reasons for them starting are constantly disputed

programme.desc: David Dickinson travels to Oswestry, Shropshire, to value antiques. Dealer David Ford has mixed feelings about a set of Irish passports, a Toby jug and a leather bullion bag, while Brenda Haller values a Hornby train set
